Title: Identification of key enzymes responsible for protolimonoid biosynthesis in plants: Opening the door to azadirachtin production
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; triterpene synthase; conserved DCTAE motif
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae GIL77; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR; coexpression/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in fruit of Azadirachta indica
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first committed step in protolimonoid/limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AiOSC1 was identified from A. indica as a candidate limonoid-pathway OSC. 2. Expression in yeast and N. benthamiana produced a major product confirmed by NMR as tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol. 3. AiOSC1 is highly expressed in fruit, a limonoid-accumulating tissue, supporting its role in pathway entry.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the 30-carbon protolimonoid scaffold precursor tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol, enabling downstream melianol/limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome mining from NCBI SRA; differential expression analysis on A. indica transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pYES2 expression system in yeast; pEAQ-HT-DEST1 in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as the scaffold-producing partner for CYP functional assays even though CYPs were from other species
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point gene for heterologous reconstruction of limonoid biosynthesis and engineering insecticidal limonoids such as azadirachtin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Insecticidal activity; bitterness in citrus; potential pharmaceutical activity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; triterpene synthase; conserved DCTAE motif
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae GIL77; GC-MS; tissue metabolite-expression correlation by qRT-PCR and LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in petiole and root, corresponding to tissues with higher melianol and salannin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first committed step in protolimonoid/limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaOSC1 grouped phylogenetically with AiOSC1 and CsOSC1. 2. Expression in yeast generated tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol. 3. MaOSC1 expression correlates with melianol and salannin accumulation in M. azedarach tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the triterpene scaffold precursor for protolimonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with tissue expression data, supports role in first step of limonoid biosynthesis in Meliaceae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate entry enzyme for pathway reconstitution in host plants or microbes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole and root for pathway-related metabolites in M. azedarach
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Insecticidal and other bioactive limonoid functions

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; triterpene synthase; conserved DCTAE motif
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae GIL77; GC-MS; coexpression evidence from transcriptome mining
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with CsCYP71CD1 and CsCYP71BQ4 in public citrus transcriptome data
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first committed step in protolimonoid/limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsOSC1 was identified from Citrus sinensis as an OSC homolog in the limonoid-associated clade. 2. Expression in yeast produced tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol. 3. Coexpression with citrus CYP homologs supports conservation of the early pathway in Rutaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the precursor scaffold for citrus protolimonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome annotation GCF_000317415; transcriptome coexpression mining
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports pathway conservation between Meliaceae and Rutaceae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing citrus limonoid pathways in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Citrus tissues not directly tested in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with AiOSC1 in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS; purification and NMR of product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to MaOSC1; higher in petiole and root
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early tailoring enzyme but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP71CD2 was identified from a unique M. azedarach CYP71 subclade and coexpression context. 2. Coexpression with AiOSC1 converted tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol to dihydroniloticin by introducing C23 hydroxylation and C24-25 epoxidation. 3. In combination with MaCYP71BQ5, it enabled formation of melianol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol toward protolimonoid formation and is required for melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts sequentially, likely before MaCYP71BQ5, based on stronger substrate consumption
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidative enzyme for reconstructing melianol biosynthesis in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs two oxidations on tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol, introducing a secondary alcohol at C23 and an epoxide across C24-25 to form dihydroniloticin

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with AiOSC1 in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS; purification and NMR of product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to MaOSC1; higher in petiole and root
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tirucalla-7,24-dien-21,3β-diol; functionally implicated C21 aldehyde for melianol formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early tailoring enzyme but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP71BQ5 was identified as a coexpressed/homologous CYP candidate. 2. Coexpression with AiOSC1 introduced C21 oxidation to form tirucalla-7,24-dien-21,3β-diol. 3. Together with MaCYP71CD2, it enabled spontaneous hemiacetal ring formation to yield melianol, suggesting effective C21 aldehyde formation in pathway context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides C21 oxidation required, together with MaCYP71CD2, for melianol formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although isolated product had a C21 alcohol, pathway logic suggests the enzyme produces a C21 aldehyde in vivo/assay context to permit hemiacetal formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required partner enzyme for engineering melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C21 position of tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol, yielding a C21 alcohol/aldehyde state that cooperates with MaCYP71CD2-generated oxidations to drive hemiacetal formation

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS detection of sequential products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with CsOSC1 and CsCYP71BQ4 in public citrus transcriptome data
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dihydroniloticin intermediate en route to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP71CD1 is a citrus homolog of MaCYP71CD2. 2. Functional analysis in N. benthamiana supported sequential production of intermediates from tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol to melianol. 3. It supports conservation of early limonoid biosynthesis in Rutaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes early oxidations required for citrus melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact individual product was inferred by homology and sequential LC-MS pattern rather than full NMR characterization in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful homolog for citrus pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitterness and bioactivity of citrus limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely performs C23 hydroxylation and C24-25 epoxidation of tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol analogous to MaCYP71CD2

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS detection of sequential products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with CsOSC1 and CsCYP71CD1 in public citrus transcriptome data
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C21-oxidized tirucallane intermediate en route to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP71BQ4 is a citrus homolog of MaCYP71BQ5. 2. Functional analysis with CsCYP71CD1 in N. benthamiana supported sequential conversion of tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol to melianol. 3. It demonstrates conservation of the early protolimonoid pathway in citrus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides C21 oxidation required for citrus melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred by homology and combinatorial assay rather than isolated intermediate NMR in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful homolog for citrus pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitterness and bioactivity of citrus limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes C21 oxidation of tirucalla-7,24-dien-3β-ol analogous to MaCYP71BQ5, enabling melianol hemiacetal formation with CsCYP71CD1
